series_title:
Parental Notification
Proceedings Cost and Fees
Payment Orders
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
Fiscal Year End plus 4 years
comments:
Docket sheets must be maintained
as required by the Cooperative
Agreement between the Office of
Court Administration and the Office
of the Attorney General.

retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Official docket sheets are
maintained by district clerk
pursuant to Local Schedule DC:
Records of District Clerk.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
legal_citation:
Tex. Gov’t Code Sec.
52.046(c); Walker v.
Stefanic, 898 SW2d
347 (San Antonio,
1995)

series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by an
agency on external entities, on the agency
by other entities, or on the agency by
internal auditors, including reports
received by state auditor’s office.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor’s Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas state agencies.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by an OCA employee to
document his or her work activities
may be a state record and subject
to this retention period.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by
agency.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under Rule 12.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
NOTE: OCA is not subject to the
Public Information Act (Open
Records Act). It is subject to Rule 12
of the Rules of Judicial
Administration.

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are wholly or partially exempt
under Rule 12.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Tx State Library
and Archives Commission.
Organization charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Fiscal notes prepared on proposed
legislation and related correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Surveys returned by the customers or
clients of an agency, and the statistical
data maintained rating an agency’s
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
See records series item 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers, or
reports delivered in conjunction with
agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations
given by or on behalf of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review.

series_description:
Instructional materials developed by an
agency for training entities or individuals it
regulates or serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance
with Sections 2054.095 and 2056.002,
Govt. Code
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
Record Copy: Human Resources

series_description:
Official agenda and minutes of state
boards, committees, commissions, and
councils.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission. NOTE: The Texas
Judicial Council, the Task Force on
Indigent Defense and the Judicial
Committee on Information
Technology as judicial agencies are
not subject to the Open Meetings
Act. However, all records created by
OCA employees for these entities
and other judicial boards that
would ordinarily fall under Records
Series Numbers 1.1.058, 1.1.060,
1.1.061, 1.1.062 will be maintained
in accordance with this retention
schedule.

series_title:
Meetings, Certified
Agendas or Tape
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Certified agendas or tape recordings of
closed meetings of state boards,
commissions, committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
See note at agency item # OCA-
2600.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an actual
meeting.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
See note at agency item # OCA-
2600.

series_description:
Minutes or notes and supporting
documentation taken at internal agency
staff meetings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Have legal counsel review before
furnishing to public.

series_description:
Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency’s appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: The FE+3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records series
in this schedule if the records series
is needed for documentation of
agency performance measures.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) - Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing non-
fiscal reports.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. See
especially item 1.1.064.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency’s
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency’s programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or by consultants under
contract with an agency that are not noted
elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are
archival when they deal with
significant aspects of the agency's
programs. For agencies that have
had an archival appraisal, separate
this records series by each type of
archival coding, A, R, or E. See page
ix for more information.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to the
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
comments: CAUTION: See item 1.1.064.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency’s programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes. The records may be
maintained with related information
including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code Sec. 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated as
a party on the agency’s behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071

series_description:
Agency level documents authorizing final
disposition of records under a certified
records retention schedule.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
Record Copy: Records Management
Officer

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions to an agency form or
any associated design or design
modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Agency copy. Includes documentation of
certification and approval - forms SLR 105C
and/or other forms approved by the State
Records Administrator.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained by the State and
Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Agency copy (includes RMD 101, TX-R-5,
306-58-1, Agency Storage Forms). Forms
indicate records transferred to storage or a
transfer of legal custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
destroyed or transferred to the Archives
and Information Services Division, Texas
State Library, showing records series title,
dates of records, and date destroyed or
transferred.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years
comments:
Record Copy: Records Management
Officer

retention_statement: Until Superseded
Until Superseded plus 1 year
comments:
Record Copy: Records Management
Officer

series_description:
Also includes documentation for disaster
recovery services provided by other
entities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
comments:
Record Copy: Records Management
Officer

series_description: Agency copy of form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
One copy of each state publication as
defined in the intro of the Texas State
Records Retention Schedule, except a
publication that is subject to a different
retention period in this schedule. For
example, a meeting agenda (1.1.058) also
meets the definition, but it must be
retained permanently; items 1.1.004,
1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003,
which also meet the definition, are closely
associated with the appropriations process
and must be retained AC+6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code 441.101-
441.106). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will retain
a copy of all state publications
submitted to it on a continuing
basis, subject to periodic evaluation
to determine if the publication
merits further retention. For
additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the Internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
§§3.1-3.16).

series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to,
background material, copy (drafts), original
artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc.
This includes all work performed both
inside and outside the agency.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Successive and
substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival. Major
is defined by both the publication's
authorship and its impact on Texas
and Texans. Original artwork,
including photo prints and
negatives that have significant value
as evidence of agency programs as
well as the potential for re-use, may
be archival.

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records
and associated processing files. Examples
include, but are not limited to, data tables
of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC
to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_description:
Automated software application and
operating system files including job control
language, program listing/source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records. *
See Legal Counsel for security
related and proprietary exceptions.

series_description:
Records of software defects and/or
enhancements to Software Programs
supported by OCA. This includes a short
description, long description, and status of
the defect/enhancement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records. *
See Legal Counsel for security
related and proprietary exceptions.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment
control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Documentation describing how a system
operates and how to use the system such
as user guides, system specifications, input
and output specifications, and system flow
charts; program descriptions and
documentation such as program
flowcharts, change notices, and other
records that document modifications to
computer programs; and data
documentation necessary to access,
retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in
an automated system such as a data
element dictionary, code book or table,
and other records that explain the
meaning, purpose, structure, logical
relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits
such as files or reports showing
transactions accepted, rejected,
suspended, and/or processed; history
files/tapes; records of on-line updates to
application files or security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Electronic files or automated logs created
to monitor computer systems such as
internet server log files, syslogs, etc.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
Medium=Electronic. CAUTION:
Does not include technical
documentation of procedures
necessary for reading or processing
of electronic records. See record
series item number 2.1.009.

series_description:
Information verifying the quality of system,
hardware, or software operations including
records of errors or failures and the loss of
data resulting from such failures,
documentation of abnormal termination
and of error free processing, checks of
changes put into production, transaction
histories, and other records needed as an
audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Medium=Paper and electronic. *
See legal counsel prior to release.

series_description:
Records of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of
user-specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state-
owned computer.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Internet cookies are erased by IT
staff during computer upgrades and
maintenance. They may be erased
by employees when they are no
longer of any administrative value.
Agency equipment is set to
automatically erase history files
every 20 days. They may be erased
by employees when they are no
longer of any administrative value

series_title:
Software Registration,
Warranties and License
Agreement
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: *See legal counsel prior to release.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Applies to all employment contracts
regardless of date of execution,
renewal or amendment.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include criminal
history checks. SEE agency item
number OCA -8200.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of employee
grievances against personnel policies,
working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U.
S. Department of Labor. SEE record
series item number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those which may
affect pay, status, or tenure. May include
cause for demotion, suspension, or
dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. May also
be used to document evidence of
employee self-improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communications.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the records
of an employee including pay grade,
position classification, employee number,
evaluation date, and termination of
employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employment Eligibility,
Documentation or
Verification of
series_description: Federal Reporting Form (INS I-9)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS 1-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for the 3 year retention period.

series_description:
Retention period applies if an agency
replies to the sender of a resume that it
will be kept on file should future job
openings occur.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
SEE item number 3.1.014 for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description:
Summary of applicant qualifications,
evaluation basis for selection or rejection,
original applications, job assignments,
promotions, separations, layoffs,
terminations, compensation records,
conditions of work, time records, hours of
training provided (separate from labor),
minority status, and gender of all selected
and rejected applicants.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

comments: *See legal counsel prior to release.

series_description:
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees’ gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.
comments: *See legal counsel prior to release.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
Until Superseded
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Reports providing statistical information
on labor force.

Minimum information needed to verify
employment, includes, name, social
security number, exact dates of
employment, and last known address.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

series_title:
Reimbursable Activities,
Requests, and
Authorizations to Engage
in
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name;
staff hired during a month; detailed listing
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
associated with in-house training of agency
personnel on personnel policies and
procedures and other policies and
procedures that govern an agency’s
programs, services, or projects.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 2 years

series_description:
Includes documentation used to complete
EEO reports.

Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status
information for each position.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee's hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Required by General Appropriations Act
(100 Day Report).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
CAUTION: If an agency does not
produce a biennial or annual
narrative report as described in
item number 1.1.066, then these
annual financial reports must be
marked with Archival Code A. The
archival requirement, when a
biennial or annual narrative report
is not produced, is met by sending
the required copies of the reports
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
This series documents state, federal, and
other sponsored grant projects
participated in or administered by state
agencies. It includes grant authorization
records, which provide evidence of the
award of grants to or by agencies;
grant/project financial or performance
reports, which are periodic reports of
financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by agencies.

comments:

may vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Records in this
series may be subject to
Government Code 441.1855.
Agencies must ensure that records
are retained for the appropriate
retention period.

supporting documentation must be

(see Legal Citations for applicable
orders) provided to RPJ.

procedures, are not subject to
